title: Autodesk 3ds Max File Import-Export（.max）
description:
Autodesk 3ds Max File Import-Export for Blender

Seamlessly bridge the gap between Blender and Autodesk 3ds Max with this powerful import/export addon.
This professional-grade tool enables direct workflow integration between two of the industry's most popular 3D applications by providing native .max file support in Blender.

✨ Key Features

🔄 Bidirectional File Support

  • Import .max files directly into Blender with full scene integrity

  • Export Blender scenes to .max format for 3ds Max compatibility

  • High-fidelity data transfer ensures models, materials, and animations are preserved

⚙️ Advanced Import Options

  • Selective asset import: models, lights, cameras, splines, animations, materials, armatures

  • Automatic coordinate system adjustment (X-axis 180° rotation option)

  • Scale conversion support (0.01x scaling for unit compatibility)

  • Customizable default import settings for a streamlined workflow

🎯 Smart Export Controls

  • Filter export by object type: meshes, lights, cameras, curves, animations

  • Selection-only export for targeted asset transfer

  • Preserve material assignments and animation data

  • Maintain scene hierarchy and object relationships

🔧 Intelligent 3ds Max Detection

  • Automatic registry-based detection of 3ds Max installations

  • Support for multiple 3ds Max versions (2020–2026+)

  • Flexible Python path configuration

  • Comprehensive error logging and troubleshooting

💼 Professional Workflow Integration

  • Drag-and-drop .max file support for instant imports

  • Comprehensive preference system with per-project settings

  • Detailed logging system for debugging and workflow optimization

  • Windows platform optimized with full registry integration

🖥️ Technical Specifications

  • Blender 4.2+ compatibility

  • Windows platform support

  • Requires Autodesk 3ds Max installation (2023+)

  • Uses native 3ds Max Python API & MAXScript for reliable conversion

  • Ensures broad compatibility and preservation of scene features

🎯 Perfect For

  • Studios working with mixed 3ds Max/Blender pipelines

  • Freelancers collaborating across different 3D applications

  • Asset conversion and migration projects

  • Educational environments teaching multiple 3D platforms

  • Technical artists optimizing cross-platform workflows

🚀 Why Choose This Addon?

This addon eliminates the complexity typically associated with 3ds Max file conversion, providing a one-click solution that maintains scene integrity, material assignments, and animation data.

Whether you're migrating existing projects or establishing a cross-platform workflow, this tool provides the reliability and functionality demanded by professional 3D production environments.

👉 Experience seamless interoperability between Blender and 3ds Max – download now and streamline your 3D workflow today!
documentation:
AUTODESK 3DS MAX FILE IMPORT-EXPORT ADDON

DOCUMENTATION

TABLE OF CONTENTS

  1. Overview

  2. Installation

  3. System Requirements

  4. Configuration

  5. Importing 3ds Max Files

  6. Exporting to 3ds Max

  7. Advanced Settings

  8. Troubleshooting

  9. FAQ

  10. Support

1. OVERVIEW

The Autodesk 3ds Max File Import-Export addon provides seamless integration between Blender and Autodesk 3ds Max through native .max file support.
The addon uses intelligent conversion to maintain scene integrity, materials, animations, and object hierarchies during file transfer.

Key Features:

  • Direct .max file import/export

  • Selective asset filtering (models, lights, cameras, splines, animations)

  • Automatic 3ds Max installation detection

  • Drag-and-drop file support

  • Comprehensive logging system

  • Professional workflow integration

2. INSTALLATION

Standard Installation:

  1. Download the addon .zip file

  2. Open Blender → Edit > Preferences > Add-ons

  3. Click Install... and select the .zip

  4. Enable the addon under Autodesk 3ds Max File Import-Export

  5. Configure the 3ds Max Python path (see Configuration)

Manual Installation:

  1. Extract the addon into Blender’s addons folder:

    • Windows: %APPDATA%\Blender Foundation\Blender\[version]\scripts\addons\

  2. Restart Blender

  3. Enable the addon in Preferences > Add-ons

3. SYSTEM REQUIREMENTS

  • OS: Windows 10/11 (64-bit)

  • Blender: 4.2+

  • 3ds Max: 2023+ (any edition)

  • Python: Compatible with 3ds Max installation

  • Disk Space: 50MB+ for temp files

  • RAM: 4GB minimum (8GB recommended)

Notes:

  • Requires a working Autodesk 3ds Max installation

  • Linux/macOS are not supported

  • Admin privileges may be required on first setup

4. CONFIGURATION

Initial Setup:

  1. Open Preferences > Add-ons

  2. Expand Autodesk 3ds Max File Import-Export settings

  3. Click Auto Detect (locates 3ds Max Python path)

  4. If detection fails, manually browse to:

    • C:\Program Files\Autodesk\3ds Max 2025\Python\python.exe

    • C:\Program Files\Autodesk\3ds Max 2024\bin\python.exe

Default Import Settings:

  • Models (enabled)

  • Lights (enabled)

  • Cameras (enabled)

  • Splines (enabled)

  • Animations (enabled)

  • Materials (enabled)

  • Armatures (enabled)

  • Apply X-Axis 180° Rotation (disabled)

  • Apply 0.01 Scale (enabled)

5. IMPORTING 3DS MAX FILES

Method 1: File Menu

  1. File > Import > Autodesk 3ds Max (.max)

  2. Select .max file

  3. Adjust import options

  4. Click Import

Method 2: Drag & Drop

  1. Drag a .max file into Blender viewport

  2. Options dialog appears

  3. Configure settings → OK

Import Options:

  • Models, Lights, Cameras, Splines, Animations, Materials, Armatures

  • X-Axis 180° Rotation (coordinate fix)

  • 0.01 Scale (unit conversion)

Tips:

  • Large files may take minutes

  • Check System Console for progress

  • Ensure free disk space

  • Close heavy applications during import

6. EXPORTING TO 3DS MAX

Standard Export:

  1. File > Export > Autodesk 3ds Max (.max)

  2. Choose destination/filename

  3. Configure export options

  4. Click Export

Export Options:

  • Models, Lights, Cameras, Splines, Animations

  • Selection Only (optional)

Process:

  1. Blender generates intermediate data

  2. 3ds Max converts to .max

  3. Temp files cleaned automatically

  4. Final file saved

Best Practices:

  • Apply transforms before export

  • Use standard materials

  • Test with simple scenes

  • Verify exported file in 3ds Max

7. ADVANCED SETTINGS

Logging System:

  • Logs stored at: Documents\blender_3dsmax_log.txt

  • Includes conversion steps, errors, performance data

Cache Directory:

  • Stored at: Documents\blender_3dsmax_cache\

  • Auto-cleaned after operations

Performance Optimization:

  • Use SSD for faster processing

  • Increase virtual memory for large scenes

  • Close unnecessary applications

  • Monitor system resources

8. TROUBLESHOOTING

Common Issues:

  • "3ds Max Python executable not found"
    → Verify installation, use Auto Detect, or set manually

  • "Export failed"
    → Check object validity, unsupported types, or isolate exports

  • "3ds Max script execution failed"
    → Verify installation integrity, free disk space, restart Blender/3ds Max

  • "Import shows empty scene"
    → Enable filters, check file integrity, review logs

  • Conversion too slow
    → Reduce scene complexity, close apps, split large scenes

  • File too large
    → Optimize textures, reduce polycount, simplify animations

9. FAQ

Q: What versions of 3ds Max are supported?
A: 2020 and later (highest installed version auto-detected).

Q: Can I use this without 3ds Max installed?
A: No. A working installation is required.

Q: Are materials preserved?
A: Yes, with enabled option. Fidelity depends on material systems.

Q: What about animations?
A: Keyframes are preserved. Procedural data may need adjustments.

Q: Batch conversion supported?
A: Not yet. Files must be converted individually.

Q: Why intermediate FBX?
A: Best compatibility between Blender ↔ 3ds Max.

Q: Blender extensions compatibility?
A: Yes, includes manifest for Blender 4.2+ Extensions.

10. SUPPORT

Getting Help:

  1. Review this documentation

  2. Check blender_3dsmax_log.txt

  3. Search Troubleshooting section

  4. Contact support with errors & logs

When Reporting Issues, Include:

  • Blender version

  • 3ds Max version

  • OS details

  • Error messages

  • Steps to reproduce

  • Log contents

Performance Tips:

  • Keep Blender/3ds Max up to date

  • Ensure 10GB+ free disk space

  • Close heavy background apps

  • Use wired connections for network files

Updates:

  • Check addon description for changelog & new features

Version: 1.0.0
Last Updated: 2024

Thank you for using the Autodesk 3ds Max File Import-Export addon!
